title: spinacia oleracea var. spinosa
intro: Spinacia oleracea var. spinosa, also known as bitter spinach or prickly spinach, is a variety of the spinach plant (Spinacia oleracea) characterized by its thorny leaves and stems. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Spinacia oleracea var. spinosa include its potential to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, making it a valuable herb for preventing chronic diseases. It is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be consumed raw in salads or added to soups and stews for its nutritional benefits.
